I have some failing tests and want to use hot reload to iterate faster. Can you set it up for me?

input.initialPrompt: I have some failing tests and want to use hot reload to iterate faster. Can you set it up for me?

data.reasoningText: The user wants to set up hot reload for their .NET test project so they can iterate faster on failing tests. Let me first look at the project files to understand the setup.

data.content: I can see the project — it's an MSTest project targeting .NET 8. I'll start `dotnet watch test` which will automatically re-run your tests whenever you save changes.

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

data.reasoningText:
The user wants to use `dotnet watch test` for hot reload. This is a .NET test project using MSTest. I can start `dotnet watch test` which will re-run tests automatically when files change.

Let me start it up in async mode so the user can interact with it.

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-4a286b38e89a4b249c3bcd268818efee && dotnet watch test --no-pager 2>&1", "description": "Start dotnet watch test for hot reload", "initial_wait": 30}

toolArgs.description: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

data.content: `dotnet watch test` is now running and watching for file changes. There's a compile error — `ArgumentException` needs a `using System;` directive. Let me fix that so the tests can run:
data.reasoningText: There's a compilation error — `ArgumentException` needs `System` to be imported. The test file is missing a `using System;` directive. Let me fix that and the watch will automatically re-run.

data.content:
`dotnet watch test` is running and actively watching for changes. Here's the current status:

- ✅ **Passed:** `CalculateShipping_StandardDelivery_Returns5`  
- ❌ **Failed:** `CalculateShipping_ExpressDelivery_Returns15` — expects `15.00` but gets `10.00`

The watcher is live in the background. Every time you save a file change, it will automatically rebuild and re-run the tests. The failing test expects express shipping to return `15.00`, but the `CalculateShipping` method returns `10.00` for `"express"`.

Would you like me to fix the failing test logic, or would you prefer to iterate on it yourself?
